market_summary: Week of Mar 31 - Apr 4 was dominated by the Iran-US war (Day 34+), strong US jobs data, and oil price spikes. S&P 500 rallied 3.4% for the week (best day since May on Tuesday), Nasdaq surged 4.4%, but both remain near correction territory YTD (-4% S&P). Markets closed holiday-shortened (Good Friday). Oil surged 8-11% on Apr 2 after Trump threatened further Iran escalation, with WTI above $112 and Brent near $109. Gold holds above $4,650 on safe-haven demand. BTC consolidated around $67K-71K, pressured by risk-off from Iran war. Silver at ~$72, gold/silver ratio compressed to ~64. OPEC+ met Apr 5, approved symbolic May quota increase but actual supply constrained by Hormuz blockade. US added 178K jobs in March (vs 59K expected), unemployment fell to 4.3%, wage growth slowed to 3.5% YoY. Fed rate unchanged at 3.5-3.75%, one cut expected in 2026. New steel/aluminum/copper tariffs (50% flat) signed Apr 2.

key_events_this_week.event: Q2 begins; S&P 500 wrapping up tough March, April expected better historically
S&P 500 rockets on end-of-war hopes; strong Tuesday rally (best day since May)

Trump prime-time address: Iran war to continue weeks, threatens to strike Tehran 'extremely hard'. Oil surges 8-11%. New steel/aluminum/copper tariffs signed (50% flat rate). Tesla Q1 deliveries disappoint, stock -5%

March NFP: +178K jobs (vs +59K expected), unemployment 4.3%, wage growth 3.5% YoY (lowest since May 2021). S&P 500 +0.11% to 6,582.69, Nasdaq +0.18% to 21,879.18. Treasury bonds fell on strong jobs data, traders pared rate cut bets
Good Friday - shortened/closed session. S&P 500 range 6,475-6,602

OPEC+ meeting: approved symbolic May quota increase, but actual supply constrained by Hormuz. Iran fires at Kuwait oil/power infrastructure. Trump ultimatum: Iran will be 'living in Hell' if Hormuz not reopened. Iran allows China/Russia/India ships through Hormuz selectively

key_events_next_week.event: Monday: Markets reopen after Good Friday. Asia/Europe digest OPEC+ decision and weekend Iran escalation. No major data releases.
Tuesday: US Durable Goods Orders (forecast -2.2% MoM). Trump Tuesday 8PM ET deadline/address on Iran. Watch for USD volatility.
Wednesday: RBNZ Interest Rate Decision (forecast 2.25% unchanged). Constellation Brands (STZ) and Delta Air Lines (DAL) earnings.
Thursday: US Core PCE Price Index (forecast 0.4% MoM, 2.9% YoY) - Fed's preferred inflation gauge. FOMC Meeting Minutes from March 17-18 meeting released at 18:00 UTC. HIGH VOLATILITY DAY.
Friday: US CPI Data (Core CPI forecast 0.2% MoM, 2.5% YoY). Often triggers largest intraday moves of the week. Could reshape Fed rate cut expectations.
Bank of Japan meeting (rate expected unchanged at 0.75%)

overnight_risks.detail: Trump issued ultimatum for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz. Iran fired at Kuwait oil/power infrastructure on Apr 5. Iran selectively allowing China/Russia/India ships through. Any new military escalation or Hormuz incident over the weekend could gap oil up $5-10 and hammer equities at Monday open.
OPEC+ approved symbolic May quota increase but cannot deliver actual barrels due to Hormuz constraints. Market may interpret this as supply remaining tight, supporting oil above $110.
178K jobs vs 59K expected. Treasury bonds sold off Friday. Traders paring rate cut bets. Could pressure growth/tech stocks Monday as bond yields adjust higher.

Asian markets showed some optimism on potential Hormuz shipping resumption earlier in the week. Weekend escalation could reverse this. Watch Nikkei, Hang Seng at Asia open Sunday night/Monday morning.
New 50% steel/aluminum/copper tariffs signed Apr 2. Peak consumer impact expected Apr-Oct 2026. Construction and manufacturing stocks may face pressure as input costs rise.
Core PCE (Thu) and CPI (Fri) both due this week. Hot prints could trigger major sell-off given already-elevated oil prices feeding into inflation expectations. FOMC minutes (Thu) add to volatility.

instrument_news.XAG-USD: Silver at ~$72, near multi-decade highs. Gold/silver ratio compressed to ~64 (below 20yr mean of 70), indicating silver outperformance. Industrial demand strong (solar, EV, AI data centers). Supply deficit continues. Technical: support S1 $69.48, S2 $66.28; resistance R1 $75.87, R2 $79.06. SLV trading at $65.79. War premium supporting. CoinCodex forecast: $73.05 by Apr 11. Prophecy target $120 remains active with 0.8 conviction.
instrument_news.XAU-USD: Gold at ~$4,677, holding near all-time highs. GLD holdings rose to 920 tonnes (from 840t end-2025). Net ETF inflows 78 tonnes in Jan-Feb 2026 (+73% YoY). Central bank buying on pace for 1,200+ tonnes in 2026 (~190t/quarter). JPMorgan expects $4,000-$6,300 range. Some bearish technical signals (Dark Cloud Cover, Bearish Engulfing) but fundamentals strong. Iran war safe-haven bid intact. Expected to trade $4,577-$4,761 range week of Apr 6.
instrument_news.BTC-USD: Bitcoin consolidated around $67K-71K. Rejected at $71,500 resistance multiple times. Key support $66,000-$64,000. March ETF inflows rebounded to $1.32B, early April mixed. Corporate treasuries at record (public companies hold 1.1M+ BTC, ~5-6% of supply). Strategy (MSTR) continues aggressive accumulation. Whale activity shifting to altcoins (LINK, BCH), suggesting BTC distribution phase. Risk-off from Iran war pressuring. Need sustained break above $71,500 for bullish continuation.
instrument_news.ETH-USD: Ethereum at $2,030-2,060, down ~6.5% for the week. Glamsterdam upgrade (biggest since The Merge) confirmed for June 2026: 10,000 TPS target, 78% gas fee reduction, gas limit 60M to 200M. DeFi TVL $55-68B. Harvard bought $86.8M in ETH ETF. Consolidating near $2,000 support. Upgrade is potential major catalyst but market currently focused on macro headwinds (Iran war, inflation). Mixed ETF flows.
instrument_news.SPY: S&P 500 at 6,583 after +3.4% weekly gain. Near correction territory YTD (-4%). Strong jobs data (+178K) may reduce rate cut probability, pressuring growth stocks. Week ahead: PCE Thu, CPI Fri, FOMC minutes Thu. Fundstrat expects further consolidation Apr 5-9 before bottoming. Geopolitical risk remains elevated.
instrument_news.NVDA: Part of tech sector that drove Nasdaq +4.4% weekly rally. Semiconductor stocks rallied to kick off Q2. Tariff risk on copper (50% flat rate) could impact chip supply chain costs. AI demand narrative intact.
instrument_news.TSLA: Q1 deliveries rose YoY but fell from prior quarter, stock dropped 5%+ on Apr 2. Underperforming broader market recovery.
instrument_news.MSTR: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) continues aggressive BTC accumulation. Public companies collectively hold 1.1M+ BTC. MSTR remains largest corporate holder. BTC price consolidation around $67-71K.
instrument_news.WTI-OIL: WTI at $112+, highest since 2022. Surged 8-11% on Apr 2 after Trump Iran war speech. Strait of Hormuz 90-95% shipping reduction. OPEC+ symbolic May increase but cannot deliver barrels. JPMorgan expects Brent $110 Mar-Apr, declining to <$80 by Q3 if Hormuz reopens. Goldman expects war premium intact through April.
instrument_news.BRENT-OIL: Brent at ~$109. Peaked at $126 on Mar 8. Largest supply disruption since 1973 oil embargo. OPEC+ constrained by Hormuz. If Hormuz reopens, prices could fall $30-40 rapidly.
instrument_news.PLTR: Part of tech rally week. AI/defense spending narrative supportive amid Iran war.
instrument_news.AMD: Semiconductor rally beneficiary. Monitor tariff impact on supply chain.
instrument_news.GOOGL: Part of broader tech recovery. Mega-cap tech led Nasdaq +4.4% weekly gain.
instrument_news.AMZN: Benefiting from broader tech rally. Consumer spending data mixed (strong jobs but tariff headwinds).
instrument_news.AAPL: Part of mega-cap tech rally. Supply chain tariff exposure via China remains a concern.
instrument_news.META: Social/advertising sector. Part of Nasdaq recovery rally.
instrument_news.AVGO: Semiconductor/infrastructure. AI demand tailwind, tariff headwind on copper inputs.

metals_outlook: BULLISH with near-term volatility. Gold ($4,677) supported by: (1) Iran war safe-haven demand, (2) record central bank buying (1,200+ tonnes pace), (3) ETF inflows accelerating (+73% YoY), (4) Fed on hold with inflation sticky. Risk: hot CPI/PCE data this week could strengthen USD and pressure gold short-term. Silver ($72) benefiting from: (1) gold/silver ratio compression to 64 (heading toward 50-60 range), (2) industrial demand surge (solar, EV, AI), (3) supply deficit, (4) war premium. Silver outperforming gold as ratio compresses. Key risk: PCE/CPI data Thu/Fri could cause sharp moves. Gold/silver ratio at 64 suggests silver still undervalued relative to gold historically (typical range 40-60 in bull markets). JPMorgan gold range $4,000-$6,300. Silver support at $69.50, resistance at $75.90.
crypto_outlook: NEUTRAL-BEARISH near-term, BULLISH medium-term. BTC ($67-71K) trapped in range, repeated rejections at $71,500. Iran war creating risk-off headwind. ETF flows rebounded in March ($1.32B) but early April mixed. Whale activity rotating to altcoins (bearish for BTC short-term). Corporate treasuries at record levels (1.1M+ BTC in public companies). Key support $64-66K, break below opens $58-60K. ETH ($2,030-2,060) holding $2K support. Glamsterdam upgrade (June 2026) is major catalyst: 10x throughput, 78% gas reduction. Harvard buying ETH ETF ($86.8M). DeFi TVL $55-68B. Medium-term thesis intact (BTC $100K target, ETH $4K target) but needs macro headwinds to clear (Iran resolution, Fed clarity). Watch PCE/CPI this week -- hot inflation = more crypto pressure.
oil_outlook: EXTREMELY ELEVATED with binary risk profile. WTI $112, Brent $109. Strait of Hormuz 90-95% blocked since Feb 28 (largest disruption since 1973). Iran selectively allowing China/Russia/India ships through. Trump ultimatum demanding Hormuz reopening -- escalation risk CRITICAL. OPEC+ Apr 5 meeting: symbolic May increase, but cannot deliver actual barrels through blocked strait. Peak Brent was $126 on Mar 8, now consolidating $105-115. BINARY OUTCOME: (1) Hormuz reopens or ceasefire = oil drops $30-40 rapidly to $70-80, (2) further escalation = oil could spike to $130-150. JPMorgan: Brent $110 through April, <$80 by Q3 if resolved. Goldman: war premium intact. EIA: Brent >$95 through Q2. Key watch: Trump's Tuesday 8PM ET deadline/address on Iran. Any Kuwait/Gulf infrastructure attacks could spike oil further.
sentiment_shift: CAUTIOUS OPTIMISM with elevated tail risk. Markets snapped 5-week losing streak with +3.4% S&P rally, driven by end-of-war hopes early in the week (quickly dashed by Trump's Wednesday address). Strong jobs data (+178K) is double-edged: good for economy, bad for rate cuts. Fundstrat sees bottoming process but recommends waiting for Apr 5-9 consolidation before buying. Key sentiment drivers this week: (1) Iran war escalation/de-escalation -- binary risk, (2) PCE + CPI data -- hot prints could trigger major sell-off, (3) FOMC minutes -- clues on rate trajectory, (4) tariff impact beginning to feed through. Overall market positioning is cautious -- hedging activity elevated, VIX likely elevated. Gold and oil positioning reflects geopolitical fear. Crypto sentiment neutral, waiting for catalyst. The week of Apr 6-10 is one of the most event-dense weeks of Q2.

fed_outlook.rate_expectations: Median dot: 1 cut in 2026, 1 in 2027. 7 of 19 members see no cuts in 2026. Strong jobs data reducing cut probability. Hot PCE/CPI this week could further delay any cuts.
fed_outlook.key_concern: Inflation remains somewhat elevated + oil price surge feeding through to CPI. Fed can take patient approach.

iran_war.latest: Trump ultimatum for Hormuz reopening. Iran fired at Kuwait oil/power plants Apr 5. Iran claims downing 2 US C-130s, 2 Black Hawks. US F-15E pilot rescued. Iran selectively allowing China/Russia/India/Pakistan ships through Hormuz. Diplomatic efforts facing 'mounting distrust'.
iran_war.strait_of_hormuz: 90-95% shipping reduction. Iran demands war damages payment before reopening. Selective access for allied nations.
iran_war.oil_impact: Largest supply disruption since 1973. Brent peaked $126, currently ~$109. WTI $112.

tariffs.latest: Apr 2: New 50% tariff on steel/aluminum/copper articles. 25% on derivative articles. 15% on industrial/grid equipment (through 2027).
tariffs.impact: Peak consumer impact expected Apr-Oct 2026. Global trade realignment accelerating -- other countries cutting tariffs among themselves, bypassing US.
tariffs.one_year_anniversary: Liberation Day tariffs (Apr 2025) raised effective US tariff from 2.5% to 27%.

weekly_closing_prices.note: Prices approximate as of Apr 3-4 close (Good Friday shortened session)

actionable_signals.risk: Hot CPI/PCE data could cause short-term pullback. USD strength headwind.

actionable_signals.rationale: Safe-haven demand from Iran war. Record central bank buying. ETF inflows accelerating. Range $4,577-$4,761 expected.
actionable_signals.risk: Bearish technical patterns forming. Hot inflation data could trigger USD rally.

actionable_signals.rationale: Range-bound $64-71.5K. ETF flows mixed. Whale rotation to alts. Corporate accumulation supportive long-term.
actionable_signals.risk: Repeated $71.5K rejections bearish. Iran risk-off. Break below $64K opens $58-60K.

actionable_signals.rationale: Glamsterdam upgrade June 2026 is major catalyst. Harvard buying ETH ETF. $2,000 support holding.
actionable_signals.risk: Underperforming BTC (-6.5% weekly). Macro headwinds dominant near-term.

actionable_signals.rationale: AVOID directional bets. Binary outcome: Hormuz resolution = crash to $70-80, escalation = spike to $130-150. Not a tradeable setup.
actionable_signals.risk: Maximum uncertainty. Trump Tuesday deadline is key catalyst.

actionable_signals.rationale: Snapped 5-week losing streak but near correction territory. Consolidation expected Apr 5-9. Strong jobs data mixed signal.
actionable_signals.risk: PCE/CPI/FOMC minutes triple event risk Thu-Fri. Iran escalation. Tariff headwinds.
